Response: Open-source software development kit (SDK) and middleware framework for robotics: provides communication infrastructure (DDS-based pub/sub), hardware abstraction, drivers, libraries, visualizers, tools, and community packages for building robot applications

Response: C++, Python, C, other (CMake, XML for package manifests)

Metric: Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: ROS 2 is a complete redesign of ROS 1 (Robot Operating System) to address limitations in security, real-time performance, and multi-robot support. Uses DDS (Data Distribution Service) for communication instead of a custom middleware. Has annual releases with LTS versions every two years. Latest LTS: Jazzy Jalisco (May 23, 2024, supported until May 2029).

Metric: Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Installation via apt on Ubuntu is straightforward and well-documented. The official documentation at docs.ros.org provides clear, step-by-step instructions. The only non-trivial step is manually adding the ROS 2 apt repository. Requires specific Ubuntu version matching the distribution (e.g., Ubuntu 24.04 for Jazzy).

Response: automated testing, Sphinx (documentation), Doxygen (C++ API), other (ament_lint code linting; ament_cmake test framework; colcon build system integration)

Response: The demo_nodes talker/listener tutorial worked correctly on first run. Output matched the documentation exactly. Comprehensive CI infrastructure with GitHub Actions and a dedicated build farm. REPs provide formal specifications for significant design decisions.

Response: Installation and tutorial demos completed without any errors. ROS 2 Jazzy on Ubuntu 24.04 is a stable LTS release. The pub/sub communication demos ran reliably with no dropped messages.

Metric: Does the software handle unexpected/unanticipated input (like data of the wrong type, empty input, missing files or links) reasonably? (a reasonable response can include an appropriate error message.) (yes, no∗ )
For any plain text input files, if all new lines are replaced with new lines and carriage returns, will the software handle this gracefully? (yes, no∗, n/a)
Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Error messages from the ros2 CLI are descriptive and actionable. Type checking at the middleware level (IDL-defined message types) prevents runtime type mismatches. Launch files with missing nodes raise clear errors.

Response: Discourse forum (discourse.ros.org), GitHub Issues per repository, ROS Answers Q&A site (answers.ros.org), ROS-Industrial community, ROSCon annual conference, official documentation at docs.ros.org

Metric: Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Exceptional documentation quality at docs.ros.org. Beginner, intermediate, and advanced tutorial tracks. Active community on Discourse and ROS Answers. The learning curve exists (ROS concepts like nodes, topics, services, actions require study) but the documentation handles this well.

Response: Exemplary maintainability. Annual releases with clear LTS policy. REP process provides formal governance for significant changes. Multiple active maintainers per repository. Rapid issue response time. vcstool and rosdep tools automate workspace management.

Metric: Is API documented? (yes, no, n/a)
Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Highly reusable modular architecture based on packages. Client library interfaces (rcl) available for C++ (rclcpp) and Python (rclpy). Supports composition to combine multiple nodes into a single process. Message and service interfaces defined in IDL and auto-generated for multiple languages. Compatible with ROS 1 via ros1_bridge.

Response: Excellent code quality across randomly sampled source files from rclcpp and rcl. Consistent style enforced by ament_lint. Well-structured layered architecture (rcl → rclcpp → user code). Comprehensive inline documentation enabling Doxygen generation. One of the best-organized open-source robotics codebases reviewed.
